title: hier_block mode module has wrong port connection order 2
body:
Hi @wsnyder,

Thanks for the fix, I replaced my **V3VariableOrder.cpp** with the fixed one and rebuild my verilator, and the **dut.v** case seems correct now. But after I run the below **dut3.v** case with `verilator -cc dut3.v --hierarchical --debug`.
### dut3.v
```
`timescale 1ns/1ns
module mod3(out3, in3, clock3, reset3)
    output  out3;
    input   in3, reset3, clock3;
    reg     out3;

    /*verilator hier_block*/

    always@(posedge clock3)
        out3 <= in3
    
endmodule

module dut3(out, in, clock, reset)
    input   reset, clock, in;
    output  out;
    wire    reset, clock;
    wire    out;

    mod3 u_mod3(out, in, clock, reset)

endmodule
```
In the debug file:
### Vdut3_007_linkjump.tree
```
(omit)
1:2: CELL 0x2004440 <e446> {e20ak}  u_mod3 -> MODULE 0x1fe1050 <e476#> {d6ai}  mod3  L3 [1ns]
1:2:1: PIN 0x1feb890 <e431> {e20ar}  __pinNumber1 -> VAR 0x1fe1680 <e13> {d7aw} @dt=0@  out3 OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x1fded40 <e628#> {e20ar} @dt=0@  out [LV] => VAR 0x2002d10 <e412> {e16an} @dt=0@  out OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
1:2:1: PIN 0x2003de0 <e436> {e20aw}  __pinNumber2 -> VAR 0x1fe1c00 <e21> {d8ax} @dt=0@  in3 INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x1fdee50 <e631#> {e20aw} @dt=0@  in [RV] <- VAR 0x20029b0 <e407> {e15bb} @dt=0@  in INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1: PIN 0x2004090 <e440> {e20ba}  __pinNumber3 -> VAR 0x1fe2130 <e30> {d9ax} @dt=0@  reset3 INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x1fdef60 <e634#> {e20ba} @dt=0@  clock [RV] <- VAR 0x20026a0 <e402> {e15au} @dt=0@  clock INPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
1:2:1: PIN 0x2004340 <e444> {e20bh}  __pinNumber4 -> VAR 0x1fe2660 <e39> {d10ax} @dt=0@  clock3 INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x1fdf070 <e637#> {e20bh} @dt=0@  reset [RV] <- VAR 0x2002390 <e447> {e15an} @dt=0@  reset INPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
(omit)
```
You can see that the order of **PIN** is `reset3 -> clock3` which is not the same as the order of the `mod3` port declaration, which is `clock3 -> reset3`. I guess this is considered as not correct? Thanks

_Originally posted by @Kelin9298 in https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/3583#issuecomment-1231258598_
